questions.text: Provide a brief description of your organisation, including any commercial relationships with Paramount and/or WBD.

Provide a brief description of the theatrical content you exhibit and which suppliers you source this content from, for example global studios (e.g. Paramount and WBD), international production groups and local producers.

Provide an estimate of the proportion of your films exhibited over the last three calendar years that were produced by each of Paramount and WBD. Explain how important you consider Paramount and WBD content is for your ability to compete, including whether there are any characteristics of either Paramount or WBD that make them a more important supplier of theatrical content, for example, higher quality or quantity of films, better commercial terms, or different distribution arrangements.
Explain the extent to which you are able to switch theatrical content suppliers (completely or partially), and which theatrical content suppliers are alternatives to Paramount and WBD.

questions.section: Questions for suppliers of free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services
questions.text: To what extent do you compete with Paramount (including Paramount- owned services, Paramount+ and Network 10) and/or WBD (including HBO Max and WBD’s pay TV services)?

questions.section: Questions for suppliers of free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services
questions.text: Explain who you consider to be your main competitors. Provide details as to whether this differs depending on the format audio-visual content is provided to consumers in (e.g. free to Air TV, subscription video on demand or other on demand or linear entertainment services), or the type or genre of audio-visual content. If possible, provide estimated viewership, subscriber numbers or any other relevant measure of market share.

questions.section: Questions for suppliers of free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services
questions.text: Provide a brief description of the audio-visual content you have acquired from Paramount or WBD, including: a. the channels through which this audio-visual content is exhibited or displayed (e.g. free to air, SVOD, or other on-demand or linear formats); and b. the estimated proportion of content available on your platform or service that is licensed from or produced by Paramount or WBD (in terms of number of individual titles, hours of broadcast or other relevant measure).

questions.section: Questions for suppliers of free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services
questions.text: Explain whether the content you acquire or have acquired from Paramount or WBD has effective substitutes from alternative suppliers (including content produced in-house). a. If so, please state who these alternative suppliers are. b. If you consider there are no effective substitutes from alternative suppliers, what would be the likely effect of loss of access to Paramount or WBD content on your viewership or subscriber numbers and associated revenue?

questions.section: Questions for suppliers of free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services
questions.text: Explain whether your business produces content (in addition to that acquired from third parties such as Paramount and WBD) and describe any barriers you face to increasing in-house content production.

questions.section: Questions for producers of theatrical and/or audio-visual content
questions.text: Explain the extent you consider you compete against Paramount and/or WBD in the supply of: a. theatrical content for display in cinemas; and b. audio-visual content for display on free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services. If possible, provide market shares on the basis of revenue generated or other relevant measure.

questions.section: Questions for producers of theatrical and/or audio-visual content
questions.text: Explain who you consider to be Paramount’s and WBD’s main competitors in the supply of: a. theatrical content for display in cinemas; and b. audio-visual content for display on free to air television, video on demand, or other linear entertainment services.

Provide any additional information or comments that you consider relevant to the ACCC’s assessment of the Acquisition, including any concerns on the impact of the Acquisition on competition in Australia.
